name: GATK Workflow On HPC
summary: Plan and run GATK variant-calling workflows on shared HPC systems.
description: This skill provides a conservative Slurm pattern for GATK workflows, including Java memory and temporary directory controls, reference and input checks, data staging reminders, and a dry-run-first HaplotypeCaller example.

inputs.name: aligned_bam
inputs.description: Analysis-ready BAM or CRAM file with matching index.

inputs.name: reference_fasta
inputs.description: Reference FASTA with required GATK indexes and dictionary.

inputs.name: intervals
inputs.description: Optional interval list or BED file used for a smoke test or scatter.

inputs.name: output_dir
inputs.description: Durable directory for generated GVCF, logs, and run records.

outputs.name: gatk_launch_plan
outputs.description: Reviewable command, Java memory settings, temporary directory, and input checks.

outputs.name: gvcf_output
outputs.description: Optional HaplotypeCaller GVCF output when RUN_GATK=1.

outputs.name: run_record
outputs.description: Logs and checklist for provenance, support, and reruns.

examples.title: Plan or run GATK HaplotypeCaller on Slurm

tests.description: Validate the manifest and referenced examples.
Run plan-only mode with a tiny user-owned BAM, reference, and interval; set RUN_GATK=1 only after the input and reference checks pass.
